mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that lowers discount rates and boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 4% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive despite UNRATE exceeding 4% threshold or predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and payroll employment indicates strong economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no correlation with combined INDPRO/PAYEMS growth or interaction term loses significance in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts combined with declining consumer spending (RSAFS) signals weak demand conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns during late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns remain positive despite HOUST/RSAFS declines or interaction term fails to predict downturns in validation samples.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through increased corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no correlation with GDPC1 trends or predictive power collapses during economic slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising payroll employment and falling unemployment signals a tightening labor market, boosting corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns fail to correlate with PAYEMS/UNRATE co-movements or interaction term loses significance in multivariate tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% triggers central bank tightening expectations, reducing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL exceeding 2% threshold or predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth indicates elevated manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no correlation with INDPRO trends or predictive power collapses during manufacturing downturns.
